mechanism_narrative: Sustained low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 8.0 percent, labor market slack signals weak aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate earnings, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and investment demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative median returns in more than 50 percent of cases, or interaction term loses predictive power in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, rising industrial production reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, pushing S&P 500 returns positive within one week.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative returns in more than 60 percent of recovery phase observations, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes correlate with positive returns in more than 60 percent of observations, or the negative relationship vanishes in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls crossing above 200000 additions over three months confirms robust labor demand and wage growth, supporting consumer spending and driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: High payroll growth coincides with negative returns in more than 55 percent of triggered months, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where real personal spending grows faster than real GDP indicates consumption-led expansion, which sustains corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over three months.
failure_condition: Consumption outpacing GDP predicts negative returns in more than 50 percent of quarters, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, any increase in the federal funds rate crossing above 0.5 percent raises discount rates and compresses valuation multiples, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Rate hikes above 0.5 percent produce positive returns in more than 60 percent of instances during tightening phases, or the signal loses predictive power.
